subject: Social Science (Civics)
overall_learning_objectives: Learn about the problems faced by marginalised groups
Understand the role of the Constitution in protecting rights
Know about laws that protect marginalised communities
Develop awareness about equality and social justice

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Marginalisation
student_activities: Students discuss situations where people are treated unfairly
Identify examples of inequality in society

lesson_plan.title: Marginalised Communities in India
student_activities: List difficulties faced by marginalised communities
Discuss why equality is important in society

lesson_plan.title: Role of the Constitution
student_activities: Write two Fundamental Rights that protect equality
Discuss how these rights help marginalised groups

lesson_plan.title: Protective Laws
student_activities: Discuss how laws protect marginalised communities
Write one example of discrimination and how laws can prevent it

lesson_plan.title: Government Support and Social Justice
student_activities: List ways government supports marginalised groups

lesson_plan.title: Revision and Chapter Summary
student_activities: Write two ways society can reduce discrimination

teacher_tip: Use real-life examples to help students understand discrimination and encourage empathy towards marginalised communities.
